hypothesis: If equivariance-inducing training regularization (such as equigrad) causes non-equivariant architectures to develop latent representations that converge toward those of architecturally equivariant models, then systematically interpolating the strength of this regularization during training will produce a monotonic increase in representational alignment with equivariant models that quantitatively predicts downstream out-of-distribution generalization performance, thereby establishing representation alignment as a causal—rather than merely correlational—indicator of physically grounded inductive bias acquisition.
